senses.text: masc. personal name, from Latinized form of Hektor, name of the Trojan hero, oldest son of Priam and Hecuba, in the "Iliad," from Greek hektor, literally "holder, stayer;" an agent noun from ekhein "to have, hold, possess" (see scheme (n.)). As a proper name it is rare in England but used in Scotland to render Gaelic Eachdonn. Heck for short.
